analyst_tape.note: Stock trades 13.6% ABOVE the mean analyst target ($121.62 vs $107.15). This is a meaningful priced-for-perfection signal: even with a moderate-buy consensus, the stock has run through the street's central estimate. Mizuho's $150 target is the sole bull above price.

post_earnings_pattern: BEAT-AND-RAISE with unanimous target hikes but wide spread. Q1 2026 (May 4): revenue $1.513B beat $1.490B est., non-GAAP EPS $0.64 beat $0.61 est., gross margin 38.5% third consecutive quarterly expansion. Q2 2026 guide: $1.535-1.635B revenue, non-GAAP GM 38-40%. All 10+ firms that commented raised targets. The spread ($80-$150) and fact that 15 of 27 analysts remain Hold/Neutral despite the raise wave reveals the market debate: recovery is confirmed, but whether the 114% YTD move has already discounted it is contested.
street_thesis_one_line: Onsemi is a cyclical power-and-sensing semiconductor company at an earnings trough, bottoming as automotive SiC content ramps (55% share of new EV models at 2026 Beijing Auto Show), AI datacenter power demand accelerates (revenue doubling YoY), and the new Treo analog platform opens a $36B TAM; the bull case is that normalised non-GAAP EPS recovers to $5+ by FY2028, making the current 28x NTM P/E cheap on through-cycle earnings.

estimate_artifact_check.flag: CONDITIONALLY CLEAN — with important context. The $4.315 NTM EPS is consistent with a forward blended consensus spanning roughly Q3 2026 through Q2 2027 (i.e., the next 12 months from mid-2026). It is NOT the FY2026 calendar-year consensus, which is closer to $2.90-$3.20 non-GAAP. The arithmetic is internally consistent: $121.62 / $4.315 = 28.2x P/E as stated. However, Daedalus must NOT use $4.315 as a FY2026 EPS figure — it should be treated as a mid-cycle forward number that blends current-year ($2.90 non-GAAP) with expected FY2027 recovery (~$5.50). FY2026 GAAP EPS is far lower (Q1 GAAP was -$0.08 due to amortisation and restructuring charges, vs non-GAAP $0.64). Flag: USE NON-GAAP NTM CONSISTENTLY; do not mix GAAP and non-GAAP.
estimate_artifact_check.fy2026_nonGAAP_eps_consensus: ~2.90-3.20 (full year, non-GAAP; based on Q1 actuals $0.64 + Q2 guide midpoint ~$0.72 + H2 recovery trajectory)
estimate_artifact_check.ttm_pe_note: TTM P/E of 89.4x is inflated by one-off charges in the downcycle (FY2025 net income $121M on prior-peak revenues); treat as distorted and ignore for valuation

q1_2026_actuals.note: Q1 marked the first YoY revenue growth quarter since Q2 2023 — management called it 'a clear inflection point'. Gross margin 38.5% is the third consecutive quarterly expansion from the ~33% trough in mid-2025.

q2_2026_guidance.note: Guidance excludes $30-40M of non-core revenue exits (intentional divestiture of ~$300M in non-core 2026 revenue). Adjusted organic growth midpoint ~+7% QoQ — above seasonal. No specific non-GAAP EPS guidance range recovered from public sources; Q2 consensus ~$0.72.

reverse_multiple.ntm_eps_note: Blended forward consensus spanning ~Q3 2026-Q2 2027; NOT FY2026 calendar estimate
reverse_multiple.implied_5y_revenue_cagr: ~8-10% from FY2025 trough ($6.0B to ~$9-10B by FY2030)
reverse_multiple.implied_peak_nonGAAP_eps: ~$5.50-6.00 by FY2028 (prior peak was non-GAAP ~$5.80 in FY2023, the market assumes a full return to peak and then modest growth beyond)
reverse_multiple.implied_wacc: ~9-10% (consistent with beta 1.98, risk-free ~4.3%, equity premium ~4.5%; Daedalus should build segment-level WACCs with each own CAPM derivation)
reverse_multiple.street_reconstruction: The street is paying 28x a forward blended EPS of $4.32 that it expects to reach as the company recovers from a severe downcycle (gross margin 33% trough back to 45%+ normalised). The bull case — the one that justifies $125-$150 targets — requires: (1) revenue recovering to $8-9B by FY2028 at 18-20% operating margins, (2) AI datacenter revenue doubling to ~$1B+ annually by FY2027 as GaNEXUS and Treo platform scale, and (3) SiC automotive content maintaining ~55% share of new EV model wins while the EV market resumes growth post-2025 softness. On those assumptions, FY2028 non-GAAP EPS of $5.50+ at 22-25x = $120-137 fair value, making the current price roughly fair on an optimistic base.
reverse_multiple.point_of_disagreement: The key contested variable is whether gross margin can recover to 45%+ (FY2023 level) given: (a) deliberate fab capacity investments made at cycle peak are now underutilized (utilisation rates drove the margin collapse), and (b) SiC competitive pricing pressure from Infineon/STMicro. The holds/neutrals (Goldman $80, Barclays $100) are not bears on the business — they believe the recovery is real but that $121 already prices in the full margin recovery. The bulls (Mizuho $150) argue margin will EXCEED prior peak as the Treo and GaNEXUS portfolio shift mix toward higher-margin products.

sentiment.institutional_note: Institutional ownership >100% reflects short interest and timing of reporting windows, not a data error — normal for a heavily traded large-cap semicon
sentiment.technicals_note: Stock is 78% above its 200-day MA and within 10% of its 52-week high. This is not a mean-reversion setup — the stock has moved from deep-trough ($44.56) to within striking distance of prior highs ($134.92) in a single 12-month run. The 15% gap above the 50-day MA suggests near-term momentum is stretched but trend intact.

market_share_2023.source: TrendForce (June 2024) and multiple market research reports (2024-2025)

sic_competitive_position.key_dynamics: onsemi gained 2 ranking positions in one year (2022→2023) through aggressive LTA wins with EV OEMs. The 2024-2025 EV demand slowdown hit hard — revenue decline from $8.3B to $6.0B reflects automotive/industrial inventory correction, not share loss. The SiC market itself grew but onsemi's utilisation fell as fab investments (Czech Republic) came online during a demand trough. SiC competition is intensifying: STMicro expanded; Infineon moving to 200mm wafers for cost reduction; Chinese domestic SiC suppliers (BYD internal) emerging as medium-term risk.
sic_competitive_position.ev_content: 120kW rack AI content $9,500; 800V EV traction inverter content $115,000 — the EV remains the largest content opportunity per unit but requires EV cycle recovery

runway_comment: Near-term sentiment is aggressively positive: the stock has returned 130% in 12 months, trades through the consensus analyst target, and attracted a wave of post-Q1 target hikes. The September 2026 analyst day (flagged by Citi's 90-day catalyst watch) is the next major catalyst, where management is expected to present updated long-term revenue and margin targets — a reset of the through-cycle earnings power thesis. The key sentiment risk: 18 of 27 analysts remain Hold/Neutral despite the raise wave, suggesting the crowded-long positioning and premium-to-consensus price create asymmetric downside if Q2 revenue comes in below the $1.585B midpoint or gross margin expansion stalls. Q2 earnings (August 3) is a near-term binary: a second consecutive beat extends the rally; any shortfall risks a sharp sentiment reversal from an already-stretched technical position.

market_lean.basis: Unanimous post-Q1 target hike wave, first YoY revenue growth in 3 years, AI datacenter doubling, SiC model wins at 55% of new EVs. Lean is bull but contested: 15/27 analysts are holds/neutrals, stock is 13.6% above consensus target, and the Simply Wall St model plus Goldman's $80 target represent a meaningful sceptical minority. The bullish headline narrative is valid; the valuation is the fault line.
